heading: Definitions

children.heading: AIS
children.label: 1 AIS
children.text: The term “AIS” means Automatic Identification System (as defined in section 164.46 of title 33, Code of Federal Regulations, or a similar successor regulation).

children.heading: Combined Maritime Forces
children.label: 2 Combined Maritime Forces
children.text: The term “Combined Maritime Forces” means the 33-nation naval partnership, originally established in February 2002, which promotes security, stability, and prosperity across approximately 3,200,000 square miles of international waters.

children.label: i
children.text: the area within a zone established by a maritime boundary that has been established by a treaty in force or a treaty that is being provisionally applied by the United States; or

children.label: I
children.text: a zone, the outer boundary of which is 200 nautical miles from the baseline from which the breadth of the territorial sea is measured; or

children.label: II
children.text: if the distance between the United States and another country is less than 400 nautical miles, a zone, the outer boundary of which is represented by a line equidistant between the United States and the other country.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of coastal States, a line coterminous with the seaward boundary of each such State (as described in );section 1312 of title 43

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, a line that is 3 marine leagues from the coastline of the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico;

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of American Samoa, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, and the Northern Mariana Islands, a line that is 3 geographic miles from the coastlines of American Samoa, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, or the Northern Mariana Islands, respectively; or

children.label: iv
children.text: for any possession of the United States not referred to in clause (ii) or (iii), the coastline of such possession.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: C Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph may be construed to diminish the authority of the Department of Defense, the Department of the Interior, or any other Federal department or agency.

children.heading: Food security
children.label: 4 Food security
children.text: The term “food security” means access to, and availability, utilization, and stability of, sufficient food to meet caloric and nutritional needs for an active and healthy life.

children.heading: Global record of fishing vessels, refrigerated transport vessels, and supply vessels
children.label: 5 Global record of fishing vessels, refrigerated transport vessels, and supply vessels
children.text: The term “global record of fishing vessels, refrigerated transport vessels, and supply vessels” means the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations’ initiative to rapidly make available certified data from state authorities about vessels and vessel related activities.

children.heading: IUU fishing
children.label: 6 IUU fishing
children.text: The term “IUU fishing” means illegal fishing, unreported fishing, or unregulated fishing (as such terms are defined in paragraph 3 of the International Plan of Action to Prevent, Deter, and Eliminate Illegal, Unreported and Unregulated Fishing, adopted at the 24th Session of the Committee on Fisheries in Rome on ).March 2, 2001

children.heading: Port State Measures Agreement
children.label: 7 Port State Measures Agreement
children.text: The term “Port State Measures Agreement” means the Agreement on Port State Measures to Prevent, Deter, and Eliminate Illegal, Unreported, and Unregulated Fishing set forth by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, done at Rome, Italy , and entered into force , which offers standards for reporting and inspecting fishing activities of foreign-flagged fishing vessels at port.November 22, 2009June 5, 2016

children.label: A
children.text: whereby the flagged vessels of which actively engage in, knowingly profit from, or are complicit in IUU fishing; and

children.label: B
children.text: that is willing, but lacks the capacity, to monitor or take effective enforcement action against its fleet.

children.label: A
children.text: that is at high risk for IUU fishing activity or the entry of illegally caught seafood into the markets of countries in the region; and

children.label: B
children.text: in which countries lack the capacity to fully address the illegal activity described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Regional Fisheries Management Organization
children.label: 10 Regional Fisheries Management Organization
children.text: The term “Regional Fisheries Management Organization” means an intergovernmental fisheries organization or arrangement, as appropriate, that has the competence to establish conservation and management measures.

children.label: A
children.text: means marine finfish, mollusks, crustaceans, and all other forms of marine animal and plant life, including those grown, produced, or reared through marine aquaculture operations or techniques; and

children.label: B
children.text: does not include marine mammals, turtles, or birds.

children.heading: Transnational organized illegal activity
children.label: 12 Transnational organized illegal activity
children.text: The term “transnational organized illegal activity” means criminal activity conducted by self-perpetuating associations of individuals who operate transnationally for the purpose of obtaining power, influence, or monetary or commercial gains, wholly or in part by illegal means, while protecting their activities through a pattern of corruption or violence or through a transnational organizational structure and the exploitation of transnational commerce or communication mechanisms.

children.label: A
children.text: collect catch from multiple fishing boats;

children.label: B
children.text: carry the accumulated catches back to port; and

children.label: C
children.text: deliver supplies to fishing boats, which allows fishing vessels to remain at sea for extended periods without coming into port.
